vulnerability.title: Verify Group Who Owns /var/log Directory
vulnerability.description: To properly set the group owner of /var/log, run the command: $ sudo chgrp root /var/log
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: newgroup="" if getent group "0" >/dev/null 2>&1; then newgroup="0" fi if [[ -z "${newgroup}" ]]; then >&2 echo "0 is not a defined group on the system" else find -P /var/log/ -maxdepth 0 -type d ! -group 0 -exec chgrp --no-dereference "$newgroup" {} \; fi

triage_decision.reason: Changing the group owner of /var/log to root is a simple file permission adjustment that does not affect authentication, networking, or boot processes. The remediation uses a standard chgrp command and is unlikely to cause lockout or service disruption.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Incorrect group ownership could allow non‑privileged users to modify or read log files; Potential audit compliance issue if group is not root
